contract_version.description: Contract version the session was created against.

verification_session_id.description: The verification session this event belongs to.

additionalProperties.description: Consented claim value. Claims are strings, booleans for derived age gates, or null when a selected optional claim has no value.

claims.description: Only the claims the user consented to share, keyed by claim identifier.

selected_field_keys.description: Canonical list of claim keys the user consented to share.

failure_code.description: Reason Kayle could not confirm the session. Failed-session payloads do not include claims, biometrics, or risk scores.

nfc_tries_used.description: How many NFC chip-read retries the session consumed before terminalizing.

liveness_tries_used.description: How many liveness retries the session consumed before terminalizing.

outcome.description: High-level outcome for the user. Cancelled sessions never produce a verified user.

reason.description: Why this terminal cancelled event was emitted. `cancelled_after_failed_check` indicates the user or relying party cancelled while a retry budget was already partially consumed. The `privacy_cancelled_after_terminal_*` reasons replace a previously-queued failed or succeeded webhook whose payload was scrubbed by a privacy request before delivery.

nfc_tries_used.description: How many NFC chip-read retries the session consumed before cancellation.

liveness_tries_used.description: How many liveness retries the session consumed before cancellation.

limit.description: The maximum number of items returned.

has_more.description: Whether there are more items available after this page.

next_cursor.description: Cursor to use as `starting_after` to fetch the next page of results, or null if there are no more items.

SessionAnalyticsSummary.description: Overall session counts for the organization.

date.description: UTC date bucket in YYYY-MM-DD format.
UTC date bucket in YYYY-MM-DD format.

trigger_type.description: The type of object that triggered this event.

trigger_id.description: The ID of the object that triggered this event (e.g. a verification session or attempt ID).

created_at.description: The time the event was created.

webhook_endpoint_id.description: The ID of the webhook endpoint this delivery targets

status.description: The status of the webhook delivery

last_status_code.description: The last HTTP status code received from the endpoint, if any.

attempt_count.description: The number of attempts made to deliver the webhook

last_attempt_at.description: The time the last delivery attempt was made, if any.

payload_expires_at.description: When the encrypted payload expires for manual retry/replay.

payload_scrubbed_at.description: When the encrypted payload was scrubbed, if no longer stored.

payload_retention_reason.description: Why the payload is retained or why it was scrubbed.

redirect_url.description: Optional URL to redirect the user to after the verification session is completed. Must use https:// (http:// is only accepted for localhost in development).

required.description: Whether this field is required by the RC.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for requesting this field. Required for most claims; may be empty for an `age_over_X` claim when `date_of_birth` is also requested in the same session.

share_fields.description: Optional map of requested share fields keyed by claim key. Each entry must include `required` (boolean) and `reason` (non-empty string, max 200).

webhook_endpoint_id.description: Optional webhook endpoint ID or endpoint ID list to target for this session. When omitted, events fan out to every enabled subscribed endpoint.
The ID of the webhook endpoint this delivery targets

webhook_encryption_key_id.description: The ID of the encryption key used to encrypt the payload for this delivery

status.description: The status of the webhook delivery

attempt_count.description: The number of attempts made to deliver the webhook

next_attempt_at.description: The next time this delivery should be attempted, or null if ready to send.

payload_expires_at.description: When the encrypted payload expires for manual retry/replay.

payload_scrubbed_at.description: When the encrypted payload was scrubbed, if no longer stored.

payload_retention_reason.description: Why the payload is retained or why it was scrubbed.

last_status_code.description: The last HTTP status code received from the endpoint, if any.

last_attempt_at.description: The time the last delivery attempt was made, if any.

created_at.description: The time the delivery was created

updated_at.description: The time the delivery was last updated

id.description: The ID of the webhook endpoint

organization_id.description: The ID of the organization that owns this endpoint

name.description: An optional display name for the webhook endpoint.

labels.description: Tag-style purpose labels for this endpoint.

url.description: The URL of the webhook endpoint

enabled.description: Whether the webhook endpoint is enabled

subscribed_event_types.description: The event types this endpoint is subscribed to. Supported values: verification.session.succeeded, verification.session.failed, verification.session.expired, verification.session.cancelled.

undelivered_payload_retention_hours.description: How long Kayle retains encrypted payloads after terminal delivery failure.

created_at.description: The time the webhook endpoint was created

updated_at.description: The time the webhook endpoint was last updated

disabled_at.description: The time the webhook endpoint was disabled, null if enabled

signing_secret.description: The webhook signing secret. This value is shown only once.

name.description: An optional display name for the webhook endpoint.

labels.description: Optional tag-style purpose labels for this endpoint.

url.description: The URL of the webhook endpoint. Must use https:// (http:// is only accepted for localhost in development).

enabled.description: Whether the endpoint should be enabled immediately. Defaults to true.

subscribed_event_types.description: The event types this endpoint should receive.

undelivered_payload_retention_hours.description: How long Kayle should retain encrypted undelivered payloads after terminal delivery failure.

id.description: The ID of the webhook encryption key

webhook_endpoint_id.description: The ID of the webhook endpoint this key belongs to

key_id.description: The key identifier, used as `kid` in the JWE header

jwk.description: The public JWK used to encrypt webhook payloads

is_active.description: Whether this key is active for new webhook deliveries

created_at.description: The time the key was created

updated_at.description: The time the key was last updated

disabled_at.description: The time the key was disabled, null if still active

key_id.description: The key identifier to use as `kid` in the JWE header.

jwk.description: The public JWK for encrypting webhook payloads.

algorithm.description: The JWE algorithm to use for webhook delivery.

key_type.description: The JWK key type for webhook delivery.

signing_secret.description: The current webhook signing secret for the endpoint.
The rotated webhook signing secret. This value is shown only once.

name.description: Updated display name for the webhook endpoint.

labels.description: Updated tag-style purpose labels for the endpoint.

url.description: New URL for the webhook endpoint. Must use https:// (http:// is only accepted for localhost in development).

enabled.description: New enabled state for the webhook endpoint.

subscribed_event_types.description: The updated event subscriptions for the endpoint.

undelivered_payload_retention_hours.description: How long Kayle should retain encrypted undelivered payloads after terminal delivery failure.

200.description: Successful operation. Returns a list of verification sessions for the organization.

id.description: The ID of the verification session

status.description: The status of the verification session

failure_code.description: Terminal failure code when status='failed'. Null in any other state.

nfc_tries_used.description: Number of NFC chip-read failures the session has consumed. The session terminalizes on the 3rd failure.

liveness_tries_used.description: Number of liveness check failures the session has consumed. The session terminalizes on the 3rd failure.

contract_version.description: Version of the session share contract.

required.description: Whether this field must always be shared.

reason.description: Reason shown to the user for this field.

source.description: Whether the field came from defaults or RC request.

share_fields.description: Effective normalized share fields used by this session.

redirect_url.description: The URL to redirect to after the verification session is completed, if provided by the integrator.

webhook_endpoint_id.description: The webhook endpoint or endpoint list selected for this session, or null when events fan out to all enabled subscribed endpoints.

verification_url.description: The URL that the platform should send the user to in order to complete the verification. Includes the one-shot `cancel_token` as a query parameter so the verify browser / native app can pass it back when cancelling.

cancel_token.description: One-shot token required by `POST /v1/verify/session/:id/cancel`. Returned only on session creation.

expires_at.description: The expiration time of the verification session

completed_at.description: The time the verification session reached a terminal state (i.e., succeeded, failed, expired or cancelled), or null if not yet terminal.

created_at.description: The time the verification session was created

updated_at.description: The time the verification session was last updated

document_type_code.reason: Needed to know the document type code

post.description: Delivered as a JWE-encrypted POST body when Kayle confirms an attempt. Verify the `X-Kayle-Signature` header against the encrypted body before decrypting.

requestBody.description: The decrypted JSON payload. The actual request body delivered to your endpoint is compact JWE.

2XX.description: Return any 2xx response to acknowledge receipt. Non-2xx responses are retried while the encrypted payload remains retained.

post.description: Delivered as a JWE-encrypted POST body when Kayle cannot confirm an attempt. This payload contains no claims, biometrics, face scores, or risk scores. Verify the `X-Kayle-Signature` header against the encrypted body before decrypting.

requestBody.description: The decrypted JSON payload. The actual request body delivered to your endpoint is compact JWE.

2XX.description: Return any 2xx response to acknowledge receipt. Non-2xx responses are retried while the encrypted payload remains retained.

post.description: Delivered as a JWE-encrypted POST body when a session expires without a confirmed attempt. Verify the `X-Kayle-Signature` header against the encrypted body before decrypting.

requestBody.description: The decrypted JSON payload. The actual request body delivered to your endpoint is compact JWE.

2XX.description: Return any 2xx response to acknowledge receipt. Non-2xx responses are retried while the encrypted payload remains retained.

post.description: Delivered as a JWE-encrypted POST body when a session terminates without producing a verified user. Emitted by the authenticated cancel endpoint, the public cancel endpoint, or as a privacy-withdrawal replacement for a `succeeded`/`failed` payload that was scrubbed before delivery. Verify the `X-Kayle-Signature` header against the encrypted body before decrypting.

requestBody.description: The decrypted JSON payload. The actual request body delivered to your endpoint is compact JWE.

2XX.description: Return any 2xx response to acknowledge receipt. Non-2xx responses are retried while the encrypted payload remains retained.
